inputs.document: The city council voted 7-2 on Tuesday to approve a $4.2 million plan to expand the downtown bike-lane network by 12 miles over the next three years. Supporters say it will cut commute times and emissions; two members objected to the cost during a budget shortfall.

Researchers at Lakeside University reported that a new battery chemistry retained 92% of its capacity after 1,500 charge cycles in lab tests, roughly double the lifespan of comparable lithium-ion cells. The team cautioned that manufacturing at scale remains unproven.

Quarterly earnings for Meridian Foods rose 8% year over year to $310 million, driven by strong demand for its frozen-meal line. The company lowered its full-year outlook, citing higher shipping costs and a slowdown in its European segment.

A regional storm dropped 6 inches of rain over 18 hours, flooding several low-lying neighborhoods and prompting voluntary evacuations. Emergency crews rescued 14 people from stranded vehicles; no injuries were reported and power was restored to most areas by morning.

The open-source project Halcyon released version 3.0, rewriting its core scheduler for a reported 40% throughput gain and adding native support for ARM servers. Maintainers warned that two legacy plugins are no longer compatible and must be migrated.

A study tracking 5,000 adults over ten years found that those who walked at least 7,000 steps a day had a 50-70% lower risk of early death than those who walked fewer than 4,000. The authors stressed the link is correlational, not proof of cause.

The national rail operator announced fares will rise an average of 4.5% in March, the largest increase in five years, while pledging to add 200 new carriages and improve weekend service on three busy lines. Commuter groups criticized the timing.

An archaeology team uncovered a 2,000-year-old mosaic floor beneath a parking lot during a routine survey. Measuring roughly 60 square meters and depicting a hunting scene, it is among the best-preserved finds in the region and will be relocated to a museum.
